headline: Jobs growth cools sharply to 57K in June
detail: Payrolls came in at just 57,000 - well below healthy levels - with unemployment at 4.2%. This signals real economic softening, which historically hits cyclical sectors like semiconductors and enterprise tech hard. Your book is ~90% in exactly those names, so this macro headwind is directly relevant.
rec: Don't add to chip or leveraged ETF positions (SOXL, SPAL, SMH) on a day with growth scare data. Hold what you have but respect your stops - especially SOXL which is already below 200.

headline: Tesla sinks 7% despite strong deliveries
detail: Tesla dropping hard on good delivery numbers suggests the market is selling the news or repricing EV growth expectations broadly. This risk-off sentiment in high-multiple growth names can bleed into your tech holdings and weigh on QQQ and SMH sentiment today.
rec: No action needed on your holdings, but monitor QQQ (currently fine near 715 with 670 stop) - if sentiment deteriorates sharply, that stop becomes relevant faster than expected.

headline: Palantir bounces on AI advantage analyst call
detail: Palantir recovering on an AI narrative callout is a positive signal for AI-adjacent names broadly, and reinforces that selective AI stories still attract buyers even in a soft macro day. This doesn't directly move your holdings but is a sentiment read for the AI trade your book is tied to.
rec: You don't hold PLTR. This is a sentiment read only - no trade warranted. It does suggest pure AI stories can still rally even on weak macro days, which is marginally supportive for names like CRWD longer-term if you stabilize that position.

headline: Ford Q2 sales drop 10.3% on F-Series issues and weak EV demand
detail: Ford's sales miss and falling EV demand reinforces the consumer and industrial softening story that weak jobs data also tells. This isn't directly in your book but confirms a broad growth slowdown narrative that pressures risk assets, including your leveraged ETFs and chip names.
rec: No direct holding to act on, but this adds to the weak-growth picture from today's jobs number. It strengthens the case to avoid adding new leveraged positions (SOXL, SPAL) until macro signals stabilize.
